  ### Stacey Gabriel
  
  1y
  
    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fstacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_thank-you-dr-bertagnolli-for-joining-us-activity-7188307801344040961-QQ3h&trk=public_profile__posts_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Stacey Gabriel shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/stacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_thank-you-dr-bertagnolli-for-joining-us-activity-7188307801344040961-QQ3h)
  
  Thank you, Dr. Bertagnolli, for joining us at the Broad Institute last week, along with the All of Us leadership team! It was an absolute honor to host you all, along with the New England-region All of Us principal investigators: [Heidi Rehm](https://www.linkedin.com/in/heidi-rehm?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Sherilyn Sawyer](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sherilyn-sawyer?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Jason Vassy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jason-vassy?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Jordan Smoller](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jordan-smoller-06a63661?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , and [Elizabeth Karlson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/elizabeth-karlson-2a010715a?trk=public_profile__posts-text) . All of Us provides researchers access to one of the world’s largest and most diverse genetic datasets of its kind. Just within Massachusetts, over 790 researchers from 43 institutions are registered to use the All of Us Researcher Workbench, and more than 40 peer-reviewed publications led by Massachusetts-area researchers are available to the public at [https://lnkd.in/gg73juiN](https://lnkd.in/gg73juiN?trk=public_profile__posts-text) We are excited to continue moving this work forward.
  
  [Monica Bertagnolli](https://www.linkedin.com/in/monica-bertagnolli-0b448b44)
  
  ### Monica Bertagnolli
  
  1y
  
  [Stacey Gabriel shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/stacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_thank-you-dr-bertagnolli-for-joining-us-activity-7188307801344040961-QQ3h)
  
  Thank you [Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard](https://www.linkedin.com/company/broad-institute_2?trk=public_profile__posts-text) for hosting me at your genome center and for partnering with the All of Us Research Program! We are so proud of its impact, especially in helping to make sure all Americans are represented in [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnih&trk=public_profile__posts-text) \-supported research.

  ### Stacey Gabriel
  
  2y
  
    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fstacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_what-an-honor-to-represent-broad-institute-activity-7175571231717388288-J0iH&trk=public_profile__posts_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Stacey Gabriel shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/stacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_what-an-honor-to-represent-broad-institute-activity-7175571231717388288-J0iH)
  
  What an honor to represent [Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard](https://www.linkedin.com/company/broad-institute_2?trk=public_profile__posts-text) at a celebration of Women’s History Month at the White House today, hosted by President Biden and Dr. Jill Biden. As President Biden said, one word sums up the United States for him:  “Possibilities”.  It was so inspiring to witness the President sign the Executive Order to advance women’s health research, which will undoubtedly create so many more ‘possibilities’ for more equitable healthcare!
  It’s been an exciting few weeks. Less than a month ago, I got to meet Dr. Jill Biden in Kendall Square, when she was there to announce the $100 million ARPA-H Sprint for Women’s Health. There’s no question that issues of women’s health have long been underfunded and understudied. Great to see the progress across the life sciences!
  
  [public\_profile\_\_posts](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/stacey-gabriel-121a9a7b_what-an-honor-to-represent-broad-institute-activity-7175571231717388288-J0iH)

  💡 “More” has become a defining theme in royalty monetization deals as late-stage life sciences companies take a fresh look at monetization as a financing option. This article continues the dialogue from our Goodwin + KPMG Symposium at [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjpm2026&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , offering deeper perspective on what’s shaping this trend.

  ### Niall J Lennon
  
  4mo
  
  [Stacey Gabriel liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/nialljlennon_last-friday-i-had-the-privilege-of-speaking-activity-7403922987609128960-0IbU)
  
  Last Friday, I had the privilege of speaking with Congressman Jake Auchincloss and NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya about the profound impact of federal investment in large-scale genomics over the past two decades. It’s remarkable to see how these initiatives have accelerated discovery and enabled genomic medicine solutions that are now being deployed nationwide through [Broad Clinical Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/broad-clinical-labs?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) .

  [Chris Lunt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/clunt)
  
  ### Chris Lunt
  
  5mo
  
  [Stacey Gabriel liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/clunt_all-of-us-anvil-imputation-service-activity-7396325182082248705-67iS)
  
  We've launched an exciting joint project with NHGRI and the Broad Institute: [https://lnkd.in/gBNcT4-6](https://lnkd.in/gBNcT4-6?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) What is genetic imputation?
  When some parts of a person’s genetic data are missing, scientists can make educated guesses about those gaps based on patterns seen in many other genomes. This process, called imputation, makes it easier to compare genetic data across people and studies.
  Here’s an analogy:
  Imagine you have maps of many towns, but some of them have large blank areas. Your goal is to figure out how far, on average, Starbucks stores are from gas stations. You’d like to use all the maps, but the missing sections could throw off your results. With imputation, you can fill in the gaps using what you know from other maps—for example, that gas stations are usually near freeway exits. By applying that rule, you can estimate what’s likely in the missing areas and make your comparison more complete and accurate.
  The better your rules are, the more accurate your gap-filling is. Greater genetic diversity gives you better rules. The best imputation server out there was produced by the University of Michigan (Go Blue!). Inspired by them we set out to make one that used even more data to give better results. This work was the child of many brilliant people: I'd like to call out my former colleague Anji Musick and NHGRI's Valentina Di Francesco for their support in making this work possible.
  
  [All of Us + AnVIL Imputation Service](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fallofus-anvil-imputation%2Eterra%2Ebio%2F&urlhash=RAjz)
  
  All of Us + AnVIL Imputation Service
